title: Ballard Power Systems Q2 2021 Earnings Call Summary
text: Ballard reported Q2 2021 revenue of $25 million, representing a 40% increase from Q1 but flat compared to Q2 2020. Gross margin stood at 15%, with adjusted EBITDA of negative $19.7 million. Cash reserves reached $1.24 billion. New orders totaled $26 million, up over 100% from Q1, with first-half orders increasing 40% year-over-year. Net order backlog was approximately $113 million. CEO Randy MacEwen highlighted significant customer wins, stating: We received an exciting order from Tata Motors...for 15 of our 8th generation fuel cell modules to power 15 buses in Delhi. Additional orders came from Solaris (13 buses in Frankfurt) and New Flyer (20 buses at AC Transit in Oakland). Over 3,300 fuel cell electric vehicles powered by Ballard technology operated in China, having completed nearly 80 million kilometers of travel. The Weichai-Ballard joint venture continues advancing despite policy delays. Europe represented over 50% of H1 2021 revenue with over 80% market share in hydrogen fuel cell buses in the European market. The company expanded into rail (Siemens Mireo Plus H trains), marine (FCwave product), stationary power, and mining applications. Backup power revenues surged 125% year-over-year. Management indicated active discussions on capital deployment and potential partnerships and acquisitions.
